summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a malicious plugin with a crafted manifest.json can successfully add sudoers rules granting passwordless sudo access and execute arbitrary commands with root privileges.
expected_security_impact: Installing a malicious plugin should add sudoers rules that grant the www-data user passwordless sudo access, demonstrating arbitrary privilege escalation and system compromise.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh successfully built the target RaspAP application, uploaded the malicious plugin manifest via the plugin installation endpoint, and verified that sudoers rules were added to /etc/sudoers.d/ granting passwordless sudo access. The attacker can now execute arbitrary commands with root privileges.
verification_method: The run script checks for the presence of the malicious sudoers file in /etc/sudoers.d/ and verifies that the www-data user can execute sudo commands without a password. This confirms successful privilege escalation.
evidence_produced_by_run: Malicious sudoers file was successfully created in /etc/sudoers.d/ with attacker-controlled content
The www-data user was granted passwordless sudo access as specified in the malicious manifest
Plugin installation executed sudo operations based on attacker-controlled manifest without proper validation
No authentication or integrity checks were performed on the plugin repository URL

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
